opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I felt completely fine, then the room just went grey, I woke up on the kitchen floor, and the back of my head is throbbing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I passed out in the kitchen and hit the back of my head when I fell, and my roommate is really worried something is wrong.

symptom_narrative.onset: suddenly, while I was standing at the bench making dinner
symptom_narrative.duration: I was only out for maybe half a minute, and I felt back to normal almost straight away
symptom_narrative.character: everything went grey and I felt lightheaded, then I was on the floor
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: scary because I actually passed out and hit my head, but I feel okay now, just anxious
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the faint itself was all over; now it's the back of my head that's throbbing where I hit it
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it was a one-off, it came on over a few seconds
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: I came round on my own within about 30 seconds and felt clear-headed again
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: I'd been standing in one spot in the warm kitchen for a good 45 minutes cooking
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: just before it, my vision went grey and I felt lightheaded; my roommate said I went really pale; no chest pain and I didn't notice my heart racing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this has happened to me before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm up and talking normally and resting on the sofa, just a bit shaken and anxious

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: I don't take any regular medications

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I'd been standing and cooking in a warm kitchen for about 45 minutes; I don't think I've had much to drink today, my mouth feels a bit dry

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants reassurance the faint isn't a sign of something dangerous, and that the bump on the head is not serious

communication_style.typical_message_style: gives a clear account and can relay what the roommate witnessed

objective_data_offered.value: roommate says I suddenly went limp and slumped to the floor, hit the back of my head, and looked really pale during it, then came round within about 30 seconds
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: bystander recollection during a frightening event; timing is approximate

objective_data_offered.value: there's a small tender lump on the back of my head where I hit it, no cut that I can see
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-inspected/felt by the patient; not a clinician examination of the head injury
